channel.title: Jsx on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Jsx on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Node.js ERR_MISSING_ESM_TRANSPILER — ESM Transpiler Missing Fix
item.description:
Node.js ERR_MISSING_ESM_TRANSPILER — ESM Transpiler Missing Fix

The ERR_MISSING_ESM_TRANSPILER error occurs when Node.js encounters an ESM file with an extension it cannot natively parse (like .ts, .tsx, .jsx) and no custom loader or transpiler is configured. Node.js natively supports .js, .mjs, and .cjs — any other extension requires explicit configuration.

Description

Common ERR_MISSING_ESM_TRANSPILER messages include:

  • ERR_MISSING_ESM_TRANSPILER: "loader" is required to use ".ts" file extensions — TypeScript without a loader.
  • ERR_MISSING_ESM_TRANSPILER: Must use import to load ES Module — wrong file extension with no transpiler.

Common Causes

// Cause 1: Importing a .ts file without a loader
import { helper } from "./utils.ts"; // ERR_MISSING_ESM_TRANSPILER

// Cause 2: Importing JSX without configuration
import App from "./App.jsx"; // ERR_MISSING_ESM_TRANSPILER in Node.js

// Cause 3: package.json "exports" pointing to .ts files
// { "exports": { ".": "./src/index.ts" } } — needs transpiler

// Cause 4: Missing TypeScript or SWC configuration

Solutions

Fix 1: Use the –loader flag with a transpiler

# For TypeScript with tsx (recommended)
npx tsx src/index.ts

# For TypeScript with ts-node
npx ts-node --esm src/index.ts

# For SWC (faster)
npx tsx --swc src/index.ts

# Custom loader with Node.js 18.18+
node --loader ts-node/esm src/index.ts

Fix 2: Use tsx as a loader for TypeScript files

# Install tsx globally or as a dev dependency
npm install -D tsx

# Run with tsx directly
npx tsx src/index.ts

# Or add as a script in package.json

{
 "scripts": {
 "dev": "tsx watch src/index.ts",
 "start": "tsx src/index.ts"
 }
}

Fix 3: Use the experimental TypeScript strip-types flag (Node.js 22+)

# Node.js 22.6+ supports --experimental-strip-types
node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts

# Enable decorators support
node --experimental-strip-types --experimental-decorators src/index.ts

Fix 4: Set up a custom loader for JSX

// loader.mjs
import { readFileSync } from "fs";
import { fileURLToPath } from "url";
import { transform } from "esbuild";

const jsxExtensions = [".jsx", ".tsx"];

export async function resolve(specifier, context, nextResolve) {
 return nextResolve(specifier, context);
}

export async function load(url, context, nextLoad) {
 const ext = new URL(url).pathname;

 if (jsxExtensions.some((e) => ext.endsWith(e))) {
 const filePath = fileURLToPath(url);
 const source = readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
 const result = await transform(source, {
 loader: ext.endsWith(".tsx") ? "tsx" : "jsx",
 format: "esm",
 });
 return {
 format: "module",
 shortCircuit: true,
 source: result.code,
 };
 }

 return nextLoad(url, context);
}

node --import ./loader.mjs src/index.jsx

Fix 5: Use a bundler for production

# Use esbuild to bundle TypeScript for production
npx esbuild src/index.ts --bundle --platform=node --outfile=dist/index.js

# Run the bundled output with plain Node.js
node dist/index.js

Examples

# Before: ERR_MISSING_ESM_TRANSPILER
node src/index.ts

# After: with tsx
npx tsx src/index.ts

# After: with Node.js 22+ strip types
node --experimental-strip-types src/index.ts

# After: with esbuild bundling
npx esbuild src/index.ts --bundle --platform=node --outfile=dist/app.js
node dist/app.js

Related Errors

  • ERR_REQUIRE_ESM — cannot require an ES module.
  • ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND — ESM import resolution failed.
  • ERR_UNKNOWN_FILE_EXTENSION — Node.js does not know the extension.
  • SyntaxError — transpiled code has syntax errors.

item.title: [Solution] TypeScript TS2762 — JSX Element Missing Closing Tag
item.description:
TS2762 — JSX element ‘X’ has no corresponding closing tag

TS2762 occurs when a JSX element is opened but never closed. JSX requires all elements to either be self-closing (<Component />) or have a matching closing tag (</Component>).

Common Causes

// Cause 1: Missing closing tag
function App() {
 return <div>Hello; // TS2762: no closing </div>
}

// Cause 2: Missing self-closing slash
function App() {
 return <img src="photo.jpg">; // TS2762: should be <img ... />
}

// Cause 3: Mismatched tags
function App() {
 return (
 <div>
 <span>Hello</div> // TS2762: </span> missing
 </div>
 );
}

// Cause 4: Unclosed component
function App() {
 return <MyComponent props={value}; // TS2762: missing /> or </MyComponent>
}

Solutions

Fix 1: Add the closing tag

// Wrong
return <div>Hello;

// Correct
return <div>Hello</div>;

Fix 2: Use self-closing syntax for void elements

// Wrong
return <img src="photo.jpg">;

// Correct
return <img src="photo.jpg" />;

Fix 3: Ensure all nested tags are properly closed

// Wrong
return (
 <div>
 <span>Hello</div>
 </div>
);

// Correct
return (
 <div>
 <span>Hello</span>
 </div>
);

Fix 4: Close custom components properly

// Wrong
return <MyComponent props={value}>;

// Correct — self-closing
return <MyComponent props={value} />;

// Or with children
return (
 <MyComponent props={value}>
 <p>Child content</p>
 </MyComponent>
);

Examples

// Example 1: Conditional rendering without closing
function App({ show }: { show: boolean }) {
 return (
 <div>
 {show && <Alert message="Warning" // TS2762
 }
 </div>
 );
}

// Fix:
return (
 <div>
 {show && <Alert message="Warning" />}
 </div>
);

// Example 2: Fragment without closing
function App() {
 return (
 <>
 <p>First</p>
 <p>Second</p>
 // TS2762: missing </>
 );
}

Related Errors

  • TS2786: ‘X’ cannot be used as a JSX component — JSX component type issues.
  • TS1128: Declaration or statement expected — broader syntax error.
  • TS1434: Unexpected keyword or identifier — unexpected tokens.

item.title: [Solution] TypeScript TS2786 — Cannot Be Used as a JSX Component
item.description:
TS2786 — ‘X’ cannot be used as a JSX component

TS2786 occurs when you use a value as a JSX component, but its type doesn’t match the expected JSX component interface. This commonly happens with React components that have incorrect prop types, missing type definitions, or when using third-party component libraries.

Common Causes

// Cause 1: Function component with wrong return type
function MyComponent() {
 return null; // Returns null, which is valid JSX
}
// But if the function signature is wrong:
const MyComponent = () => 42; // TS2786: number is not a valid JSX element

// Cause 2: Using a non-component as a JSX element
const notAComponent = "hello";
return <notAComponent />; // TS2786

// Cause 3: Missing React types
// If @types/react is not installed
const App = () => <div>Hello</div>; // TS2786 if React types missing

// Cause 4: Class component not extending React.Component
class MyComponent {
 render() {
 return <div>Hello</div>; // TS2786: not a valid React component
 }
}

Solutions

Fix 1: Ensure the component returns a valid JSX element

// Wrong
const MyComponent = () => 42;

// Correct — return JSX, null, or a valid element
const MyComponent = () => <div>Hello</div>;
const MyComponent = () => null; // Valid in React

Fix 2: Install React type definitions

npm install --save-dev @types/react

Fix 3: Extend React.Component for class components

// Wrong
class MyComponent {
 render() {
 return <div>Hello</div>;
 }
}

// Correct
import React from "react";
class MyComponent extends React.Component {
 render() {
 return <div>Hello</div>;
 }
}

Fix 4: Use the correct function component signature

// Wrong — arrow function without proper typing
const MyComponent = (props) => <div>{props.name}</div>;

// Correct
interface Props {
 name: string;
}
const MyComponent: React.FC<Props> = ({ name }) => <div>{name}</div>;

Examples

// Example 1: Forwarded ref component
const MyComponent = React.forwardRef<HTMLDivElement>((props, ref) => {
 return <div ref={ref}>{props.children}</div>;
});
// If forwardRef types are wrong, TS2786 occurs

// Example 2: Higher-order component
function withAuth(Component: React.ComponentType) {
 return (props: any) => {
 if (!isLoggedIn()) return null;
 return <Component {...props} />; // TS2786 if ComponentType is wrong
 };
}

Related Errors

  • TS2762: JSX element missing closing tag — JSX syntax issues.
  • TS2322: Type is not assignable — prop type mismatches.
  • TS2339: Property does not exist on type — accessing non-existent props.
